reviews.review_text: As big 4 we was expecting the same level of the brand but no. Was lacking In many things cleaning pool, the camp kitchen was working only one fire spot, the shower are full of web and dirty staff.

We stayed for  2 weeks and unlike other parks you dont get a discount for weekly stays.

We had an Ensuite pitch which wasn't cleaned once during our stay I had to clean it myself which when your staying in a caravan your cleaning product are limited.

Pitches were maintained and the pool was lovely. The lady who welcomed us on the first day was very helpful.

This property was so disappointing. The staff were rude older South African man and the Asian woman on reception, no customer service skills at all. The place has a smell. Our room was mouldy and musty a smell that not even a fan  would rid of. There were very minimal people around the park if any at all it was really Sad. There was rubbish around the gardens area near our room, bins were overfull we watched the same bin not be emptied for 3 days. For a Big 4, this place has no Big4 Standards.  No Big 4 Feel to this place at all. Don't bother. The other parks in Darwin are so much better, Free Spirit Resort or Discovery.
Beautiful 💜💥

I was a returning guest, I tried to make a booking but wasn’t able to, I called into the caravan park to see if I could get a site, absolutely disgusting service from the after hours staff, it was around 1730 not overly late, if you are going to work with tourists or people looking for accommodation you shouldn’t be so rude and unhelpful. Will never return to your caravan park.
I am staying in my caravan amenities are clean and tidy friendly staff easy access for all types recommended anytime

Well, I feel the various low scoring comments listed below regarding Park facilities, staff and upkeep of the caravan park are the same that I encountered.   Not sure what has turned this once a great place to stay at, to a park that I couldn't wait to leave....

This park has so much potential! The first thing that needs to be replaced, are the current staff. Customer service and doing their job, is not their strength, or focus. One particular maintenance staff member, appears to be the self appointed security officer, receptionist, gatekeeper and law enforcement agent. The park requires a lot of maintenance, with the camp kitchen, laundry area and toilets in the older section, in need of a total upgrade. For us, not a good experience.

Great caravan sites, great staff, great amenities, relaxed atmosphere, good vibes, always clean and accessible. Disabled access and facilities were great. Was there during cyclone fina and staff were very supportive and informative. Will definitely be back
I stayed here for the cyclone, they never lost power during the storm. They were so accommodating with our needs. They offer all l sorts of sites you can think of. Greatful for the service
The park is in a beautiful, tropical setting in a very convenient location close to shops and gateway to attractions throughout Darwin.
Stunning place access every where from here,stoped in the off season
Great spot to stay

We stayed in a powered site for 2 weeks. It was very hot with very little shade in the area we were but that's mostly cos the trees need to grow some more. There was a random whirly wind that took the awning off the side of our van. If I went to Darwin again, I'd choose somewhere closer to town. The staff were all lovely, and the pool was amazing. I spent a lot of time at the pool.

Amazing staff from front desk to the cafe. One of the staff, Chris, helped us reverse into our site being new caravan owners. The cafe onsite serves great food too and the customer service was brilliant. Easy access to the highway. Will return again.

Good location and clean amenities, however we paid for a premium site, but it was one the most uneven sites of our whole trip, just lucky we had plenty of blocks and where able to find extra bits of wood and blocks around the site to get level, it isn't really good enough considering what we paid!!

This park has both an established and new section.  The established area is shady whereas the new section has some trees but offers little or no shade.  The latter area is where you will be located if you have a dog.   That said the facilities in the new area are obviously new and clean and the sites are large and grassed with staff working hard to maintain cleanliness and grass.  Shower stalls are huge and water pressure is great.
The park is located about 15 min from Darwin.   It is near the airport but we had no issues with noise.
Pool area is great.
Once the trees grow in the new area it will be an all over great park
Good park close  driving distance to many of Darwins attractions

Stayed for a month, honestly it was one of the best, maybe the best campsite we’ve stayed on for a while!! The staff were friendly and approachable when we had an issue with fellow neighbours. Toilets and showers always clean! Close to Hidden valley raceway which can be loud, so if that’s not your thing take a look at when the events are on!

Issues:
1. Initially given a room and the air con did not work. We had just driven 8 hrs and arrived at almost 5pm the heat was stifling.
We called the supposed manned 24hrs a day mobile number and messaged approx. 30 times. No one answered or got back to us.
We finally found someone walking around who worked there and she swapped our rooms. The next day the whole debacle was not acknowledged at all.
2. I had left my pillow in the original room and when I realized it was day 4 of the stay and there was a rush around by the maintenance guy who found it (thank you)- it should have been given back the day i handed them the original room key after they found it. Again, nothing was spoken about. Laundry: One of the dryers doesn't work but we were charged $14 to use it via QR Code. No reimbursement.
3. No Wi-Fi in the room therefore the tv doesn't work was told " sorry it doesn't work in the rainforest retreat rooms"
4. The bed configuration is the WORST I've ever experienced, two single bases pushed together and then topped with two single mattresses! the bump in the middle felt and now have back pain. Put the bases together no problem but use ONE mattress on top!

Better management of the place is needed:
1. Reimbursement of charges should be acknowledged and automatic
2. reduction in cost to stay there for the Wi-Fi not working (no tv ) should be automatic and acknowledged.
3. TERRIBLE Bed configuration
Would definitely NOT recommend this place to anyone.

Fantastic place to stay, the staff were super friendly and helpful, and were very accommodating, we had 3 dogs travelling with us and the staff were really good. The amenities were clean the only issue for me was there were not enough working washing machines but really can't fault anything else.

With a freeway either side and under the flightpath of the airport a few km away it's very noisy. Facilities were always a bit unclean and the last night we went to the restaurant which was advertised as open every day only to find it closed. The pool is also fairly small and the week we were there never saw it cleaned the water looked dirty. Did have some shade and it was conveniently close to the MXGP which was a plus
Conveniently located within walking distance of free bus in to Darwin. We had a good pitch probably one of the better ones around the tree line.
Nice place to stay if you going to motor sports

Stayed for a week in August, on a ensuite site , We are a group of 3 Caravans, it was very average , needed up grade as the ensuite was poor in design as well ,shower made the total area wet , drainage was poor , mat supplied was older than me , the sites are small , and if you have a big van , you can’t fit your car in very easily without being on someone else site , pool was cloudy ,worst one we seen on the trip , I rang up to see if we could get in a few hours early , very nice receptionist, sure that will be fine , but one of the ensuite may not be cleaned yet if that’s ok , fine we said, We arrive and get a drilling because we were early and I said we rang and the comment was well your ensuite won’t be cleaned because your early, I said that’s ok , her comment was well I’m just telling you , Wow I thought , anyway I’m usually happy with Big 4 , but not this one , why so rude and abrupt, suggest don’t work in customer service industry if you don’t like people, work somewhere else , other receptionist was lovely and efficient,
Very helpful staff

I stayed on one of the unpowered sites, it was close to the amenities and a short walk to the pool. The toilets and showers were clean and spacious and the camp kitchen was suitable for my needs, if a bit untidy. The campground are well maintained and its location was great for getting into town and back.
Everything was very good.
Great location, very clean amenities, great pool. Staff were very accomodating and welcoming. Will be back 👍👍

It was a good place to stay
We had booked 2 premium powered sites and when we arrived late afternoon. Our sites were not together so we tried ringing the number that was texted to us and there was no answer so we perked up near our friends and then the next day when reception was open we went and discussed our situation as we wanted  to be camp next   to each other  and it was resolved
The ammenities were great
The pool could’ve done with a clean as there was scum around the edge and the camp kitchen could’ve done with a bit of a spruce up
Other wise it was a great park to stay at
Great
We had a really nice stay. The people were nice. It was very clean and very quiet!

Our stay involved tourism around Darwin and attending the MXGP. It was a handy location to the airport to catch an Uber to the airport to pick up a hire car and the free shuttle to the races was fantastic. Great pool, BBQ area and the amenities!
Thank you to the friendly staff😊 who were understanding to our vehicle getting fixed which took 2 weeks more! 😊
Loved the big 4 Hidden Valley
We stayed for a month it was pleasant, the park itself was clean and friendly and secure.

Hidden Valley Holiday Park is an excellent facility, with heaps of camping, van/camper and fixed accommodation to choose from. Located between Darwin CBD and Palmerston, it's convenient if you have transport; a little detached if not. However, it's close by the Hidden Valley Motorsport Complex for racegoers, racers and their teams. My Pandanus villa cabin is basic but very clean, well maintained and good value, with a double bed, ensuite, TV, bar fridge and coffee/tea making kit.

Prior to our arrival, staff were helpful. Unfortunately the room allocated to us was uncomfortably small. Not manageable for 2 adults.
The location is nice, facilities look ok, but we didn't want to stay after enduring 1 night in the tiny room.

Super friendly and helpful staff.
I booked an unpowered site. The girl at reception immediately asked if I wanted it in the shade (a thoughtful touch, much appreciated).
First caravan park I’ve been to with stoves in the shared kitchen (as well as BBQs and a toaster).
Clean bathrooms.
Great location.
Highly recommended!

Accommodation was perfect for my family's holiday.
Cabin was clean and had everything that we needed

Great holiday park, conveniently situated 10 minutes outside Darwin. Nice grassed sites if a little on the small side. Nice Cafe on site and refreshing pool. Amenities are kept spotless and ground staff super friendly

Paid extra for the private ensuite, but it was worse than the communal showers. Poorly designed shower-toilet combo and the camp area has zero shade.

Campgrounds were infested with mosquitos and sand flies and there’s no part of me that’s not been bitten, even through tropical grade DEET insect spray.

In saying all that, it’s in a convenient location with a well maintained camp kitchen, so take advantage of its proximity to other places and spend as little time as possible in the grounds itself.
Fully enjoyed our stay, would definitely return should the opportunity arise
A great spot to explore Darwin and the surrounds. A well kept site with friendly staff.
Good comfortable cabin.
Great place to stay. We asked for a premium spot and thats what we got. Our van was in shade all day which was great with 34c and sun all day.

The room I received did not meet our requirements. We requested a twin room and received a queen bed which did not meet our requirements as I was travelling with my son, which was explained in my booking. The business has refused to provide a refund. The floor in the room was also filthy and there was dirt still in the dust pan in the bathroom.

We enjoyed the spot we were allocated in the lower area, others suggested was dry and dusty, park staff were doing a great job watering and encouraging the grass, amenities were clean and tidy our little dawg enjoyed the place as well.
Nice green neat park, convenient location.

I had a very disappointing experience at this campground. I went twice to simply ask about the prices for an unpowered site, and both times I was treated extremely rudely by the old lady at reception. From the moment I started speaking, she looked annoyed and made it very clear that she didn’t want me there, quickly saying there was no space.

When I came back two days later with my partner to politely ask about the first available date for a site (since we work nearby), she immediately accused us of having stayed there without paying – something that was completely untrue and very offensive (is literally the first thing she told us after we asked for the price). She never greeted us, and instead of listening to our question, she cut us off saying: “You have 2 minutes, I’m not working past 4:30pm, so hurry up or I’m done.”

We told her it was fine, that we would just ask somewhere else if she didn’t have time for us, and her response was: “Perfect, no problem, I don’t care.”

That kind of attitude is absolutely unacceptable in hospitality. We left with the impression that customers are not welcome here.
Three only cause it's a bit out of the way. Can't fault it any other way.

Nice caravan park. We had a shady area which was nice considering how hot it was. Unfortunately, with the car racing so close, very noisy on Friday & Saturday nights until around 10.30pm. This was only practice, so obviously a lot louder on Drag night.

The site is generous for our 9 meter caravan and close to amenities which are modern clean and fresh. The manager is friendly and professional. The park is well located to town centre, harbour and museums.

Easy to find with decent facilities and a friendly reception to check in.

We hired a camper but the rental company hadn’t supplied a hose adaptor for the fresh water feed. Thankfully Chris (from Winchester), on site maintenance, helped us out with an adaptor and even let us take it with us to save us from the same problem in the next campsite! Would definitely return.

Noise from speedway for the Friday and Saturday night were very loud an unpleasant and on Saturday night after the speedway finished then the music started followed by the Doff Doff till 7:00 am Sunday. I don’t know why parks treat none powered customers so bad as we don’t over load the electricity usage and are put in the worst area of the park, it might as well be on a nature strip as our site looked like the storage area for caravans look better.
Friendly people and fabulous, wide site.  Thanks Team!

Terrible. We booked a cabin, so i thought, and purchased a room upgrade. Well our room has a comunal kitchen outside, my husband complained.  Receptionist said,  we had a Kettle in our room and said it was a self contained room. Very disappointed.

We understand you had issues with your phone
We were not happy with the wifi , it did not work and was very slow
It is true that we come for a holiday but these days internet is a must especially if you work and travel
It was a bit disappointing

Great spot but unfortunately has an insect biting problem. I suggested that they inform patrons so that they can take precautions . As for me I am injured and it could have been avoided by covering up and applying repellent . The response to my  suggestion was indifferent , un sim pathetic . Don’t see what the problem is with just informing people. Profit be fore welfare of there patrons is letting the big 4 name down.  Really Sad.
Clean amenities, great location, friendly staff.
Girls at reception awesome as always
On powered site, with dog, no shade was a bummer.  $60 per night for a small powered site is excessive.
Well maintained caravan park centrally located to most places
We stayed as we thought you weren't to far out of Darwin. We were able to drive into the capital and do the activities we chose just that the time seemed to go quick would love to come back some day.

Staff were not there at 2.00 o’clock in the afternoon and no signs for when they would be back amenities need maintenance electrical posts need maintenance and due to lack of are now dangerous and illegal I am a retired electrical contractor. Office staff abrupt and do not listen   your fees are high at $60 per nite and $7.00 to wash clothes 2 machines did not work.  Second big four I have stayed at. Will not stay at any others. To improve you need to realise you are in a people buisness first

Staff were very friendly and accommodating. Amenities were very spacious and clean.
The camp kitchen was very messy. Lots of peoples personal possessions strewn across every where, had obviously been there a long time. Needed binning.
The only complaint I had was that the pathways around the amenities block directed men to walk straight past the women’s toilets where they can see right in.
Would definitely stay here again.

I was on a powered site not a room. I paid for a premium site and was not advised of any bat issues. They made a mess of my caravan roof and awning. Before winding the awning in I had to clean it and was refused any asiistance by the park. I ended up washing it with my own gear which was not suitable or safe. I then paid $15 to HP clean the van.
Average park. Noisy as close to airport.  BBQ in camp kitchen not working. No hot water in shower one night and toilet block not cleaned for over 24 hrs. |Expect a bit more for the price.
Ensuite powered site.  Park and facilities were fantastic.  Bit noisy with planes and helicopters but we chose where to stay.  All good

We stayed in a powered site only.
Veryyyyyyy noisy esp late at night coz of the planes being close to the airport
Boom gate is noisy too if you stay near the gate.
The dump point is right on the main road of the van park and it stunk. Anywhere you go you have to pass this stinky dump point.
Showers were clean enough at the time of stay but you need to choose the right cubicle to get a good shower head and water pressure.
Good thing only js not far from the Mindil beach market.
Pricey for $70/night.

Disappointed that the villa we booked was given to another guest for the first night of the stay, due to staff not reading names correctly. No apologies and attitude of staff did not change.

Location of the park is great. Close to everything.
Park facilities are basic. Needs to be refurnished and new management.
Our powered site was clean all other facilities used were clean and tidy.

This is an awesome place. I found the ensuite powered site was great. It was clean and had plenty of space and powerpoints. The staff was amazing and always had a welcoming smile! Here's a shout-out to Jackie who went the extra mile to retrieve a small and insignificant item I left behind that was very dear to me. Thank you 🥰
Very clean and very nice park
Focused on money, was permitted to park an additional car inside the facility and then next day was charged $60. Park is well located but not if you want to sleep. Traffic noise is very loud

Overall we enjoyed our stay at this caravan park, however the amenities and the lack of customer service were very disappointing.|The newer shower block near our van site did not have sufficient hot water for the number of people using it so every shower we had was barely warm until we realised the other shower block (which only had 4 showers) had hot water.  The soap in the bathrooms was not refilled despite being empty for at least 5 days.  The laundry had 2 out of 4 washing machines out of order.|2 of the ladies on the front desk during our stay were very unfriendly and made it clear that handling guest enquiries was not something they wanted to do and was an interruption for them.  When I returned the gate card as we left the park, I was very surprised that the lady in the office (who I hadn't seen before during our 2 week stay) smiled, said hello and thank you!  Very different from the other 2 ladies.|Dinner at the Cafe was nice but they need better lighting outside so you can see your food|Van sites are large and the pool is lovely.  Close to Darwin

The location of this park was good, easy to drive in to Darwin and we used uber more than once and they were not too expensive . The bottom section of the park, where we stayed, is pretty crowded,  with little shade and inadequate amenities for the numbers of travellers staying there. Camp kitchen is too small, very basic and could do with more shelter. Toilets and showers were not overly clean, they were well used and needed attention from cleaners more frequently. Sometimes no soap in dispensers. We found reception staff were quite uninterested in us in general, not friendly and one lady was rude. The top section  looked lovely, pool area was nice.
Great location loved it

Reception lady was very abrupt & not welcoming at all . She was serving another customer before me & proceeded to roll her eyes as the customer wasn’t paying attention. She couldn’t find our booking & appeared very frustrated & said oh there’s just so much going on here . Turns out it was under Darnett not Garnett . She literally gave me the map with our site shown & the gate code .. that was it she didn’t give any further information about the park . First impressions are everything & from that interaction with her I wouldn’t stay there again . And the noise from the boom gate hitting the ground is very annoying as it kept us awake & woke us in the morning. We also spoke to others who said the same .
I hope this feedback is taken seriously especially the complaints about the staff as they can make or break your stay
I thought I had booked a cabin, but had a hotel room.  It was clean, had everything I needed. Holiday Park although out from the city, there was easy free bus access which ran regularly.
Great campground and very handy to city. Sites and facilities wxvellent

2 nights at Pandanus Lodge and 1 night in Powered site.
Lodge is quite noisy and far from amenities of park.
Powered site was beautiful, shady and very good amenities

Fantastic spot close to the city
Needs a little more trees 🌳 where we stayed but it is a very nice spot other than that
Very welcoming and freindly

Everything was great, nice and close to Darwin, good amenities. Only downside was the amount of noise, particularly from the maintenance staff, suggesting that if they love to use the leaf blower all day change out to an electric leaf blower.

Stayed in the lodge style accommodation, single room.  Large queen bed, bathroom with shower and toilet. Small fridge, supplied one small milk, tea, coffee and sugar for a couple of drinks.  Shampoo and conditioner, as well as soap was also supplied.  Air-conditioning and tv were in the room. Nothing was restocked for 9 nights, the bed was made and towels changed after 3 or 4 nights stay.  Floor was never mopped, even prior to arrival. Exterior of toilet was also not clean. All other spaces appeared clean apart from dust. Can be very noisy from other guests coming and going, sound travels easily.  Also can here local motor sports at Hidden Valley on some weekends. Laundry door wouldn't open, and the machine wouldn't work either,  I only attempted 1 time. Was unable to see reception as my working hours were outside of the opening hours.
Parking very close to the lodge. Pool area looked great. Approx 15 mins drive to Darwin city and 20 mins drive to Palmerston. I would stay with my caravan, or in the stand alone rooms, but not in the lodge rooms again, as a light sleeper and solo female traveller. Lodge room in dry season (very busy) for 9 nights was $1350.
We were unable to get access to our room until 4pm, despite turning up at 2.10. I was shown a sheet with the cleaning roster.

This park is ok but its just that a caravan park.
The showers in the new section guarantee a cold shower evey time without exception so expect very little in this regard.
The washing machines require an app and are a cause for frustration for many.
There are very few trees in the new section so if your van is air-conditioned ok but other wise look forward to a hot experience.
The boom gate has a mind of its own some times it works other times not.
On the positive side it's close to darwin city and if all you want is a base to explore the sites it's well located. Staff are friendly the swimming pool is ok.

Mel couldn’t have been more accommodating. Bent over backwards to get us a site, as we are a big unit. Very welcoming, facilities are clean and tidy. As the name says hidden valley, nice spot with plenty of shade & central to Darwin.Enjoying our stay.

We normally stay at Robbie Robbins Reserve but they were completely full so I tried this park and were lucky enough to get a spot, although the park is huge so I am not sure they would ever fill up. The service when I enquired about a spot was very cheery and helpful and it didn't take long to book in. I had a requirement to be out in the open because of our Starlink and that didn't seem an issue for them and our site is huge and backs onto a lovely lawn area with the amenities close by. There is a good sized pool and alongside this is a cafe that is open 7 days a week, I am not sure of opening but it closes at 8.00pm. After setting up we decided we wanted to add a few days to our stay so I contacted reception and it wasn't an issue, each time the service has been very friendly and this makes it a pleasant experience. Of course it costs more to stay here but I have to say it would certainly be first choice next time in Darwin.

The room I had was clean, spacious and comfortable.
I had only previously stayed in an ensuite powered site with my late wife and we regularly stayed here for many years, simply because Hidden Valley Holiday Park offers great value and everything anyone could want, at reasonable prices.
Facilities only just sufficient until a school group comes and then definitely not enough.  A couple of obnoxious men always hanging around.

Services poor office.staff unfriendly
We spent 3 months in the park and found it unwelcoming  we were never asked how our stay was going just once would have been nice
The swimming pool is much to small for the park and pool facilities outdated
The old camp kitchen is very poor and old and lighting below par also not water proof the new camp kitchen is ok but along way from us
The main amenities are old and in very poor condition but clean
The cafe staff were very friendly and food was very good
Location is very good can walk to a bus stop and a tavern up the road for meals
We found booking long term we paid more than the people booking shorter stays that is just by talking to people that were camped near us
Unfortunately we would not stay there again or recommend it to friends . We have already booked at a different park for next year
Overall we would rate the experience 3 out of 10..

This was a good place to stay with the family, it was clean and a good location to stay at. Yet the beds were uncomfortable. You could feel the springs through the mattress and I hurt my knee getting out of bed on one of them. The bathroom was nice, and the water pressure was next level. Overall the cabin was adequate.
Spacious, close to shopping centres and easy drive to Darwin outings .. also cheap enough to uber

We paid for a powered site. We may as well have stayed in a car park at a shopping centre, at least it may have had some shade. From sun-up until sunset we were in full sun, even the air conditioners struggled to keep up and it looks like we have cooked the compressor in our fridge. We have struck this with Big4 regularly of late and strongly suggest that you stay with another chain or private park like we intend. $60.00 per night for this is legalised theft.

Stayed for 9 nights in cabin/s - originally booked 2bdr “palm cabin” and changed to 1.5bdr “Rainforrest Retreat” when we extended… not because we were unhappy with the Palm cabin but it was already booked. Palm cabin had good sized bedrooms and huge enclosed deck, we found the living room/kitchen area a bit cramped but overall it’s a good setup. Rainforest retreat had much bigger/open living area and more modern fit out, we would book it again if we were to return. Staff at park were super helpful, we really enjoyed our stay at the Big4 Hidden Valley

Great well maintained site within close proximity to Darwin.
Really good cafe on site.

Well where do I start? We booked a powered site and a cabin. The cabin at $285 a night - a massive rip off. The most uncomfortable bed, without a doubt I have ever tried to sleep on. Pillows - what a joke. Bits of cotton wool shoved into a pillow case. Outside balcony was covered in leaves and dirt. Wouldn’t mind if it was $100 a night but this is closer to $300. No plug in the bathroom sink, no light in the fridge, uplifting vinyl in the living areas, a couch that should be thrown in the bin. Filthy and falling apart. Big4 are normally better than this. The powered site was ok, facilities had no soap in the dispensers or paper towels. I noticed this on the first day and thought they would restock it. We were there for five days and nothing changed. Dump point hand wash was also empty for the whole time. I’d never recommend this place and we have told a few travellers about it after we left. The Management needs changing. They only care about profits at the expense of providing simple fixes to basic problems

Nice camping grounds clean facilities. Well looked after facilities. Staff were helpful when I paid for dryer in wrong laundry location and refunded me. A sign in bottom laundry facilities saying dryers are in top area laundry would be useful.
Great park big sites if required, good facilities.
New area lacks shade, will be good when the trees grow

Great spot to stay for access to Darwin Harbour
Amenities lovely
Sites very cramped
We loved this Caravan Park. Stayed 11 nights.

Being close to the airport in the flight path was something we hadn’t considered, so we found that to be downsider after being in the quiet of the bush up until then. We got used to that though and realise  it was also the ‘War games’ time! Ablutions were clean and tidy and access to washing machines via an app was helpful. The cafe and pool looked lovely. There were a couple of things that would make me think twice before staying again though…
1.  I found the welcome at the office to be efficient but was disappointed at the response  of a staff member to an incident that occurred while I was checking in. A customer had not realised they needed to swipe the card even if the boom gate was up and consequently almost had it come down in their car. He was nonplussed but the staff member behind the counter acted in a rude and derogatory manner, making a comment like ‘ oh the bloody idiot’ when she saw this happen. I found this to be unprofessional.
2. We had booked a good 6 months in advance asking to be sited with or near our friends. While they had a lovely shady site, we were well away from them in a new treeless space. The siting had nothing to do with size of vans as the ones near him were much larger than ours and we would easily have fit.

We only used the park as a place to park our van and sleep really- not as a ‘holiday park’, so it met our needs in that regard and was relatively well located access-wise to the city and other local attractions.
Stayed at powered site. The amenities, laundry, camp kitchen and dump point are nearby. Public transport just outside main road and town centre easily reached.
Lovely shaded well maintain grounds. Beautiful clean ensuites and easily accommodated an extension to my stay. Highly recommend

The park was overall quite adequate with a cafe and pool and shady sites.
Unfortunately we were allocated a site on what looked to be a newer are which had a red dirt road and absolutely no shade, trees had been planted but a long way from providing shade!
There was a construction site immediately behind us causing dust everywhere when workers were using heavy machinery which was most days.
We would be giving a better review had we been allocated a better site.

We loved our time here. Amenities were spotless, camp kitchen well stocked with very clean barbies. Laundry was.good although 2 washing machines were broke when we were there. Staff at reception were lovely as were the girls in the great little cafeon site. Our site was a great size we went squashen in like sardines. I am giving this park a 10 out of 10. Well done to everyone there

We had no trees for shade, the toilets were clean but very hot sauna like. The pool was small and at the entrance to the park, the cafe looked appealing until you received your order. We ordered breakfast and coffee, breakfast was cold and coffee order was not right. Fresh juice appeared to be water with added flavouring and ice.  The park is in the flight path of domestic and RAAF planes, close to speedway track. Van sights were a squeeze and we had to move our car for neighbouring van to back up to their van. Very disappointing and will not be returning. We requested a refund as we could not tolerate more than 3 nights of our 5 night stay. No refunds provided.

Out the back of nowhere in the flightpath of domestic & RAAF planes plus the noise of the speedway nearby every night. The 'donga' showers were very hot like a sauna and a little unsavoury. No views to speak of and a long, long walk to the pool... sorry but that was our experience.  (we left two days earlier than due)
Ideal area, close to Darwin, loved the bistro, the pool and friendly staff. Happen to love the caravan site given to us.

Stayed with 22 ft caravan. Was tight fit need help to position correctly.
Noise from aircraft was constant day and night.
Close location to town and other shops makes it a good location.
Looked at all caravan parks whilst I was there and this one was the most convenient.

Recently stayed here for 3 weeks in an ensuite site. We rang two days before we were booked in to see if we could get in a day earlier. We were told that would be fine and we could get on the site whenever we arrived, as it was empty. We arrived at about 11.00 and we were told we were too early. I explained what I was told when I rang and was then told we could go on the site, but the ensuite needed to be cleaned out. When we got to our site, one of the permanents had his car parked on the site and was using the ensuite as a storage room. He was not happy when we arrived and commented that he had been here 4 years, worked at the park and it was his day off. He did move everything out of the ensuite and cleaned it, but was obviously not happy about not being told we were coming. In regard to the park, we found it very safe and quiet. When you come in the entrance it is beautiful and well maintained. However, the further you get down the back, the more untidy it gets. There are a number of permanents that have added lean to’s on and it looks a bit like a shantytown. The grass was quite long on the sites and I needed to weed the garden around the path to the ensuite as it was encroaching onto the path. The clothes line on the ensuite was bent and barely usable . There was only 1 spare role of toilet paper and when I requested more I was told that was all they supplied and if more was needed we had to supply it. Overall, it was a disappointing stay at what I believe could be a great park. As a big 4 park, I did not find it to be up to their standard. Eric and Julie Holt

Big 4 Hidden Valley, Darwin - 5/5 Stars

My recent stay at Big 4 Hidden Valley in Darwin exceeded all expectations. This caravan park stands out for several key reasons:

## Outstanding Amenities
The caravan sites feature excellent concrete pads for awning areas, providing a clean and stable foundation regardless of weather conditions. The on-suite options connected directly to your caravan are a game-changer, offering private bathroom facilities that elevate the camping experience to a new level of comfort.

## Family-Friendly Accommodation
When our family flew up to visit, they stayed in one of the palm cabins and absolutely loved it. The cabin featured two bedrooms and a comfortable lounge/kitchen area, but the highlight was definitely the large entertaining balcony nestled amongst the palm trees. This spacious outdoor area created the perfect tropical setting for family gatherings and meals together.

## Impeccable Cleanliness
The entire park is meticulously maintained. From the communal areas to the individual sites, the attention to cleanliness is evident throughout the property.

## Exceptional Staff
The team at Hidden Valley truly makes the difference. Their friendly, helpful approach ensures guests feel welcome from check-in to departure. They're knowledgeable about local attractions and readily available to address any needs.

## Overall Experience
If you're looking for a premium caravan park experience in Darwin, Big 4 Hidden Valley delivers quality accommodations in a well-maintained environment with staff who genuinely care about your stay.

Highly recommended for travelers

This Big 4 caravan park is hands down a credit to management.  We’ll definitely be back.

Definitely won't be going back there!!!!!
The room was OK , clean and adequate but very noisy, we didn't get much sleep - doors banging, loud talking until well after midnight
Toilets and amenities are clean and well looked after. Can get a little dusty on the outer edge of the park in the dry but sprinklers were there to level the dust.

Caravan pitch only .Surly receptionist who refused to give me the price as advertised on the internet.Came up with the biggest load of B.S.i have ever heard.site was good nothing special.4days then moved on.

The Big4 ensuite sites are pretty good regarding size. The ensuite is one in a cluster of four so privacy such as talking in your ensuite can possibly be heard by people if they are using theirs at the same time. The sink plug could be replaced. The ladies at the reception desk are lovely and have all the information you need re bus times ( bus stop 700m away) etc. Swimming pool looks inviting and laundry and camp kitchen is adequate.

A very big park. Good facilities and very clean. Never got to experience the pool because it was too far away. A park this size really needs 2 pools. We were on a powered site at the very back of the park, I think it may have been the overflow because it only had a dirt road. No shade there at all and our site had a mound of dirt and uncovered pipes. There was a portable shower/toilet but it was way too hot to use it. The heat in it was stifling. Up the front of the park it looks really lovely, shame we didn’t get to enjoy the beauty.

Waschraum sauber , personal freundlich , pool etwas klein für die grösse des Campingplatzes. Sind von big 4 anderes gewohnt. Aber tolle Livemusik.
Leider kein trockner , zu wenig Wäscheleinen vorhanden, leider waren wur auf der Seite,  wo überhaupt kein Schatten ist . Wie aufm Grill...
Aber was für uns wirklich ei e Zumutung war , dass der dumping Point , der abladeplatz für die csmpingtoiletten direkt neben unserem Platz liegt , also wirklich direkt, ca 0,5m daneben   das ist super, wenn man abends noch draußen sitzt und andere ihre Toilette direkt nebenan entsorgen...
Es wäre gut , wenn nicht anders machbar ,dass es da festgelegte Zeiten gibt ...hat  schon einen komischen Beigeschmack beim Abendessen oder danach bei einem Gläschen Wein... macht Spaß

A campsite that is nothing like a Big4!!!! First of all, for the positive points, the staff is very nice and the facilities are clean.
But then the rest: Some sites are in full sun, you would think you were in a supermarket car park, The caravans are stacked on top of each other,  the swimming pool is small, the children's play area is tiny.But the worst, worst, worst is the noise!!! You are next to a Super 8 circuit (until 10 p.m. sometimes) and during the day in the campsite, the garbage truck beeps and the person who collects (or rather pushes) the leaves with a machine  several times a day!! Horrible..
A great Big4 park. Premium site was convenient and everything works.
Great location and great place to stay  only problem is the airport close by.
Facilities and kind staff, the only big minus was that we booked 2 lots and they placed us in very far apart places and would not move us closer
Nice park , a little noisy with the jets going overhead,close to Darwin town center.

I highly recommend this place.
From the moment I made the reservation until the time of my departure, that establishment and its delightful staff ensured that my brief stay there was flawless and pleasurable.

I have stayed at this park a number of times. In cabins caravan and camping while on contracts in Darwin.
I always get surprised at the negative reviews for this park as I find the staff accommodating and thoughtful.
They go absolutely above and beyond to cater for the needs of the clients. I have seen this first hand.
The grounds are kept well amenities are well looked after and camp kitchens are great to cook at.
I love Hidden Valley has always been a home away from home for us.
We are grateful for the kindness of the manager Danny and her team.
Forever grateful 🙏

My partner and I stayed here in our caravan. Our cat was an acceptable pet as long as she was leashed and we picked up after her.
The camp kitchen is a gazebo type structure.
We used the ensuite type amenities and the amenities near the pool. The water pressure was good with plenty of hot water.
The pool looked inviting.
Out front there is a shelter as a pick-up point for organised excursions. A great idea.
The staff are very helpful when planning excursions.

We were parked in the shadey area of the park (ensuites). Good location. However. We had issues with the hot water for the ensuite. Luke warm showers at night and cold in the morning. When we booked last year we still had a van without an ensuite. Luckily our new van has one and we had to use it instead. We arrived Friday and reported on Sunday. Gave it a chance. It was still faulty by Tuesday and reported again. Wednesday afternoon and it's a warm shower.
We have solar ourselves and know it can be hotter.
Maybe the van that shares this system is using more than necessary.
Maybe it will be sorted before we leave on Saturday.

Friendly staff and accommodating. The cabin was clean. It was a little tight for the bed as there was only one access side unless you climb from foot of the bed. The shower was roomy however the basin and toilet pedestal was a little squeezed for room.
The location was very good for traffic noise.
Breakfast was hearty and dinner.
No hot water for stay, but a $250 refund was given back. Well Done!!!

We had been travelling for a couple of weeks and looking forward to a room with a hot shower, use the camp kitchen with all the appliances and relax in front of the tv for a bit. However none of this was working. When I pointed this out to reception I was told “that can’t be right there are some big hot water systems up there. Heaps of hot water for everyone”.  No apology, no rush to fix it. Just “we’ll get someone to have a look when they can”.

Excellence is what they deliver, going above and beyond the five-star experience, making them a hidden gem. We were warmly welcomed at the car by a charming and helpful staff member who had all the necessary documents streamlining our arrival process. Our spacious and shaded site provided the perfect setting for a relaxing stay, and we found a floor mat already secured to the ground allowing for a seamless setup, complemented by a sparkling pool and spotless amenities. This park goes above and beyond to ensure a tranquil and enjoyable experience for all guests, even during peak seasons. It really is a true gem, maintained with precision, and offering a peaceful atmosphere, so treat yourself to exceptional service and make unforgettable memories at this little slice of heaven!
Extremely comfortable stay. Amenities exceptional and always service with a smile.
We don't often stay in caravan parks but this one was great.  Very clean, very quiet.
Not enogh fridges
No road noise, tucked out of the way on a road that wasn't busy. Great rooms, comfy with all the necessities, very freindly and helpful staff.

No communication skills from front office staff. Booked 2 ppl in on a powered tent site when I'm only 1 person, they claim they ALWAYS book 2 ppl in even when there's only 1, why? plus there's a lot more room on the unpowered tent site but they claim they've reached capacity. Huh? Never again will I get conned like this. The backpackers charge $100 less per week & you get get aircon in the room compared to sweating your gutz out in a tent

Horrible place. Welcomed by a guy who said read this, your checked in, go that way. Handed us a piece of paper and a card. I said what's our site number, he heavy breathed and pointed on a number on the catd. In the hundreds. How dare I not know that. Tiny site looking over construction site on dirt road. More dust and dirt than anywhere driving from down south. Tiny sites and we had a small van. Awnings out, pegged down 1/2 metre between next van. Cars on top of each other. Could hear a whisper or the guy snoring in site next to us. They locked 2 demountable toilets during the day, I guess to stop people using them during the day. Saw guy unlock it about 4. Noisy!! if you like motor or bike car racing noise then it's the place for you.  Freeway noise. Domestic and military planes flying very low 24/7.. we found other caravan parks in Darwin not by motor racing circuit,  airport, industrial construction site. Larger sites and much cooler. Near shops, pubs, cooler. It is hot as hell, no shade. They were putting uncovered vans, tents, motor homes against fences, just anywhere, for "unpowerd " sites. It was a depressing place and so many other options. This is the only Caravan park review I have ever written.  But we rely on reviews.  There is no where to walk that isn't a pure industrial area. Do your homework.  We failed on this one, however a friend of a friend recommended it..In my defence a close friend said not to,  and the team we are travelling with decided hidden valley.  They were very or are very upset on the recommendation.  They Just kept complaining and said just so depressing. Other people left when they drove in. What ever owner answers with this is exactly our experience.

Recently spent a week at the park in a caravan. We decided to opt for a premium shaded site due to the heat.  Unfortunately these sites were on the same road as the maintenance sheds.  There was  constant movement all day along with garbage and laundry trucks.  Staff were using what look like old golf carts that were noisy and spewed more smoke than an east german Trabant. Appears the park also caters for some sort of respite accommodation.  I caught one of these clients lurking around our van at 10pm.  I spoke to the staff the next day who didn't seem to care and said to call police.  Fairly disappointing for the price.

We spent 6 nights on a powered site in our Campervan from 12/6/24. From the moment we arrived and were greeting by an enthusiastic lady who checked us in without fuss, they were very busy as it was Supercars races weekend. The next morning, the same lady want out of her way to assist us working out easiest way to get to the CBD, highlighting bus routes and providing directions. Then on Saturday after attending the car races when the shuttle bus failed to arrive within 45 minutes of scheduled time, I rang the Park to see if they could provide any info on the service, the lady I spoke too said she was due to close the office in 10 minutes, if she hadn’t heard back that the bus had arrived, she offered to drive to the venue and transport us in her private vehicle! Talk about customer service, amazing and appreciated. Of course the amenities, cafe and general upkeep of the Park was exceptional. Thanks for making our Darwin stay very enjoyable🤗

Rooms a bit ordinary
Ensuite less than conventional

Goede lokatie, maar de foto's op internet xien er mooier uit dan de werkelijkheid. Pak je een normale kampeer plek dan betaal je veel voor op een ongezellig veld vol in de zon te staan. Voor de mooie plekken in t groen, meer betalen.
Just got here today ! So far  excellent

A really lovely, peaceful and tidy place with very kind and helpful staff. We planned on staying a bit closer to Darwin for a few days but decided not to because we loved this place so much! Bus stop just up the road for very easy access to Darwin city too.

Overall the park was alright but I think I was just in the less nice area. We had a drunk man roaming and yelling down the halls at all hours of the night that didn’t make it feel very safe. But then the facilities were great when we left the area surrounding our room.
Great location and ease of getting to the city. Clean pool and bathrooms. Wi-fi was awesome
Great location,  very quiet but not too far to travel to Darwin city.  Ensuite sites clean and well maintained.  Very helpful,  friendly and welcoming staff. Highly recommended.
Very peaceful relaxing holiday park had a great time here close to airport and shops hope to visit again soon

Stayed at an ensuite powered site. Loved the lockable toilet and shower, awesome idea. The site was easy to back into, the park itself was nicely kept and set in a nice quiet location just back from the main highway. Staff were lovely. Only thing that came to mind during our stay is that a bin/skip bin at the rear of the park would be beneficial, not a big drama as we just took our rubbish out with us and dumped in the bins at the front whenever we drove out, but one a bit closer by would have been good.
Overall, we had a great time and will definitely stay again.

Un lieu où nous avons sejourné une nuit. Nous avons été upgradés. Arrivée tardive donc les clés étaient dans un coffre dont le code nous a été communiqué dans la journée. Bungalow propre est assez grand. Accueil aimable.
Awesome spot hidden away in lush greenery. There is something for everyone on this site. From caravanning and  tenting to rooms to villas. Friendly staff.

Staying at this holiday park for a couple of months. Nice clean pool. Amenities are very clean and spacious. Staff are lovely. Playgrounds, BBQ's all around the ground. Nice clean laundry 👌 would stay here again.

Not only are the rooms excellent quality, it is a bargain price at this time of year !
Excellent location for me in between Darwin and Palmerston but what makes the best impression for me is the manager Dani, so warm and welcoming !
Thank you, will recommend !
A Place to stop but not to holiday at. Needs more shade and space.

We can’t recommend this park highly enough!
The park is spotless, staff absolutely amazing nothing was too much trouble.
We have stayed here before and will definitely stay again.

Came in just to have a browse around the shop as I’m working on the new overpass. Was hoping to find a crossword book and had exhausted all the fuel stations in the vicinity. The man that served me went out of his way to find me one. Great service. Many thanks

Checkin expierence was great

Immediately got into my room that wreaked of paint to the point of causing a headache.

Then noticed later that I was unable to close my blind due to there being no turning pole, guess I'm not getting a sleep in.

TV is no bigger than an iPad screen, for near enough $400 for 2 nights this is disgraceful
Great spot. Great people. Living the dream!

Great location in Darwin.  The room was great . In walking distance Down the road from the Midway hotel .
The room was clean & tidy .
Fridge , coffee making , Air conditioner, on-suite bathroom.
My room was pretty modern.

I have certain quality standards when I think of Big 4. Half of the park would meet these standards (just) and the other half absolutely would not. Half of the park is a tree-less waste on the border of some kind of huge construction site. No shade. Lots of dust and because of no shade it feels very close to your neighbour. We moved from this part of the park to the older part which has lots of grass and trees. We also felt ripped as we only needed an unpowered site and were pushed into a powered site because the unpowered site was too hard to find by ourselves after 5pm.... also why does reception close at 5pm? Isn't this the peak time when people are turning up? We did have a quick look at the other caravan parks in Darwin and they didn't seem much more attractive. If the others were better I would have given this one 1 or 2 stars. Oh, this one is very close to the motor door complex so excellent engine noise on the days it's operating

We were anxious about staying here due to the negative reviews! Well, all I can say is if you choose to stay near an airport…. Don’t piss and moan about the planes!
The park was nice, clean amenities and good coffee food van. Two pools, what more could you ask for.
Close to the city and attractions, give this place a go. 👍🏻
Wifi not so good
Just a brilliant place to stay very central to most needs in the Darwin area and the service from the staff is very good

We arrived after hours and a maintenance man on a bike booked us in for 1 night on an unpowered site. It was $50, which is way overpriced. We were woken up at about 6am by construction directly next to our site. When I gave them feedback as leaving the lady at the counter was quick to interrupt me and say, 'you were told about the construction'. Actually no, we weren't and that doesn't take away from the fact $50 per night for an unpowered site is a complete rip off, let alone when you are right next to a massive construction site and woken up by machinery at 6am

Great time had at the park, we stayed in the Palm Cabin and had a very enjoyable time overlooking the pool area and surrounded by palms. Rooms very clean and air con worked, a must if visiting Darwin. Midgees are a pest in Darwin so bring your repellant, they'll be there. Pleasant and helpful service at the front desk with an easy check in and checkout.

Hi Dan,
my recent holiday with you was aweful: you failed to warn me you were booking us next to an active construction site.
You neglected to warn me to cover up and use repellent, but clearly had an established biting midge( sandfly) problem on site. During my stay with you, I suffered 70+ bites to my legs with allergic reaction.
Twice-when I booked, and upon arrival, there was no mention of the construction going on! So we were subjected to horrible industrial noise for 12 hours from 630am daily, it's dust gusting over our campsite, and midge (sandfly) attacks day in, day out. Then, criticised for putting up with it for so long when we'd had enough!
A far cry from your misleading and glossy advertising, coupled with dubious commentary that's it all not your fault. Well, it is your responsibility.
Well maintained and cleaned often. Very accommodating staff. Good size sites.

Good caravan park. Clean amenities.
Good pool. Great Cafe.
Nice pub, just up the road.
Reception staff were great and managed to fit us in for a fortnight.

We stayed at the Big4 Berrimah in early August and it was one worst caravan sites I have been too.
First up we were next to a construction site which was working from Monday to Saturday  from 0630 until 1900. The noise was horrendous  and you could not escape from it unless you left to go out.  Secondly there was a huge amount of dust coming from the construction site which got into everything. Thirdly the sandflies was huge nuisance. Staff at the Big 4 should have informed of the sandfly problem so we could have protected ourselves.
Finally we wanted to move to elsewhere but we had to fight tooth and nail to get a refund because of ther 14day cancellation rule

Sites are a bit confusing and some are a bit tight to get into. Facilities are really good, as is the location bit noisy from the planes overhead. Also major roadworks going on next door at the moment which is noisy.
Very convenient spot but if you are troubled by biting insects, this isn't for you. There were also busloads of teens which made for lengthy queues for toilets and showers.
Stayed in a cabin. Beautiful small room. Cheap aswell.  Great location. The absolute friendliest staff in the world. No wonder why the place is packed!

Stayed for 3 weeks
Enjoyed our stay
Lucky to have a shady spot
Fabulous and friendly staff
Amenities excellent
Staff were really helpful for us when we hit a big hiccup with our plans in life.  Very greatful with to them.
Great amenities and shade trees In older part of park. Noise is the only issue from the free way and airport. Park is being  encroached on by industrial development.

I’ve never felt the need to leave halfway through a booking at a Caravan Park until staying at Big4 Hidden Valley.

It’s mind blowing that you’d be charged $100+ a night for a powered site that is majority dirt/weed coverage, without any shade, along a dirt (dusty) road and with a drain pipe at the rear of the site.

When booking over the phone at the start of May, we explained that we’d be a group of school students in hiking tents and what we were told, was not what we were given.

Even at check-in, my colleague was advised that we’d been allocated a ‘grassy’ site, asked if we could downsize to one site as there might be walk-ins, not offered any information (map/park info) and told to come back in the morning to fix up our payment (we arrived at 4pm and there was no one else in the waiting bay).

It was only when we challenged the above that we were told we could have ‘upgraded’ our site to shade and grass for an extra $5-10. After already smoking us so much, we’d have happily paid the extra.

The site was completely inappropriate as per our request. It was a safety hazard to have our students in that environment/heat. Even we we advised we were pulling the plug and leaving at 12noon, we were still charged for a second night… even with sites free around us indicating the park wasn’t at capacity.

I’ll certainly be looking to take my business for future trips to the Top End elsewhere.

TRES mauvais experience....
Nous avons reserve 2 nuits dans cet hotel/camping pres de Darwin, afin de visiter la ville et le KAKADU national Park en juillet 2023.
Le logement est tres petit pour 4 personnes, 2 adultes et 2 ados de 15 et 17ans. Les lits superposes sont tres petits pour des ados, les fourmis vont et viennent dans le bungalow a leurs guise. Le PIRE ? La salle de douche, extremement sale, peu pratique, tout dans la meme piece donc piece mouille apres utilisation de la douche, peu de debit, salle, insectes present du a l'ouverture sur le haut...bref on ne recommande pas du tout.
First rate park with beautiful ablutions
Clean park nice & quiet not too far out of Darwin.  Definitely stay there again
Grateful to fit me in at a busy time. Thank you. Service and amenities 👍
Ok....ein bisschen laut...Rennstrecke hinten, main Road mit Road Train Verkehr und Starts  der Flieger vom Airport Darwin...aber sonst lustiges Volk auf dam Camping...

Powered sites. They are jamed packed. Down the back there is no shade, and gravel roads. The price compared to the rest of inner Darwin is fair. Road noise isn't to bad but be prepared to be woken up from 4.00am onwards from the airport that goes straight over the top of it.

Alot of sand flies about. Bring ya repellent
Reception is quick and easy. Didn't have any issues at the park. The pool needs a clean tho they have no problem running the leaf blower around even when there is people swimming.

Big group of us stayed 3 nights across a number of cabins.  The staff were amazing - greeting us with smiles and accomodating every request.  Great facilities. Clean, tidy and the middle pub is up the road, serving awesome meals. Also cafe on site, serving coffee, bacon and egg rolls etc.   Some cabins are next to a secondary arterial road, so some minor road noise.

We stayed here In a big Group of 16..rooms were tidy on entry..bunk beds squeaky but ok to sleep on..ironing board was broken.. and always had to clear possums and cute green frogs out of the showers..which for people fearing possums at 3am toilet run..not great..maybe close in shower/toilet area..fridge in cabins are small so bring esky for extra bulky items..
apart from that staff at reception were friendly 😊
And this place was a handy location for everything we needed to do around Darwin.
Great place to stay, friendly staff, Cafe has great food.

Best and safe location to stay in Darwin and drive just 10 mins to enjoy all attractions or watch the huge sunsets from the beaches.
All staff are so helpful.
A big thanks to the groundsmen who are sprinkling continuously to keep the grass spot on underfoot on the many sites.
Thanks guys!

Booked in on campside. Unfortunately it was beside the dump point. Nobody told before.
66$ /Night for an extremely stinking place.. Thanks for that!
By the way: if people leave until 10am, don't close the showers/toilets at 9am for cleaning.
